item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a tough one for me. I like the idea, love the references but dislike some of the wording quite a bit. For example the use of the word pulverise in the description of what the coin does to anything constraining its movement seems wrong to me. Unless the coin can anomalously reduce any material to a fine dust rather than just tear free the use of the word destroy would be fine. The other part I found difficult to read was the Activation Events description. It came off to me as a near impenetrable mass of causality jargon. I got what it meant but it took a bit of rereading. In fairness that may be entirely on me. No vote from me but close to a -1. That said I would personally love to see an addendum of Activation Events.

item.title: HI! It's been a while.
item.content:encoded:
I popped back over here on a whim to find my account still active. Aces! Glad I did though, my dear death-coin had been stripped! Naked of photography! That is hopefully resolved now.

A question: if I created an Activation Log page, would y'all be down with adding to it? I have to consider the possibility that it will include massive tragedies (imagine the state of this thing on 9/11/01 or all of 2016) and it may come off as trite/disrespectful.

Feedback is appreciated because I'm squarely on the fence with this idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One or several images in this article have been removed due to non-compliance with the Creative Commons license. They may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact apoplexic or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.


This automated message was sent on behalf of apoplexic. Please do not reply to this message or send other messages to this bot. Contact apoplexic in regard to this message. If you believe that you are not the intended recipient of this message, that the image was delivered incorrectly, or have other concerns about this bot, please contact anqxyr.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can we experiment on any special effects when flipped by a D-class?

Also, I feel that what you said about Tom Smith should be noted in the article. the fact that in most occasions we couldn't know who the coin is referencing until YEARS later needs to be stressed.

Say Elon Musk, who was hospitalized as a child after being severely beaten, causes the coin to flip.

Heads, we have paypal, space X, Tesla motors, we know why the coin flipped and who it was for.

Tails, a child somewhere died, who knows, who cares, lots of people died that day, The Foundation decides it was some celebrity, after all, who's to tell who the coin flipped for?

if it was an already famous person who bites it, sure, we know. but who's to say that tails didn't show for some nobody who would have changed the world?

maybe as a researcher note or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor note, according to 1841-Ex, the Foundation was responsible for the February 3rd event.

On thing that I thought of for historical figures getting mulligans, I just thought of the many assination attempts on Hitler, especially Operation Valkyrie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I missing something or is .5m³ too small for something to be called a chamber (5 cm x 5 cm x 2 cm)?

item.title: Welll well...
item.content:encoded:
Been away a minute… glad all is still stirring here.

So I finally changed up that destiny part; i'm hoping it has a bit less clunk now.

Anyone want to find a scientific way of saying "This coin is tossed by the universe itself to potentially give special people a goddamn mulligan" , or is the now-ambiguity more appropriate?

also: addressing Euclid classification: it is not only Euclid for it's own action (although potentially destructive in a minimal way), but is also Euclid due to the worth of the information it can potentially provide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I looked for a like button, then realised we weren't on Facebook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ditto. I was with you until that line, but then I hit it and had to downvote. "Destiny" isn't a word that should be used in a document like this, because it's meaningless in a scientific sense. Predestination requires that the universe be deterministic, which has not, to my knowledge, been actually proven. And, in fact, I have no idea what that sentence means, if there's no causal or predictive relationship between the coin and the event. Are you trying to say that it's the event that causes the coin to flip and not the other way around?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The effect is neat, but the containment procedures seem tremendously overcomplicated. Why can't we just put it in a box and let it flip to its heart's content?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… Why do we care? And more pertinently, why in 682's good name is this euclid-class? The worst it can possibly do is punch a hole in boxes people put it in. It doesn't seem to have any ability to affect the universe any more than random chance does. Also why does it need to be monitored with three cameras and a clock etc.? Major overkill.
Just, why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Date: March 31, 1993

Not particularly on-topic with anything, and I know that there's an actual reason for it, but I did a double-take when I saw this because it's my brother's DOB. Anyways, I think this article is pretty neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah. So the subjects of the activation events are just really important people.

Instead of mentioning their destiny and such, could you simply say that the coin reflects the fate of people who would have probably greatly influenced human science and culture? Sounds more professional than "destiny."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm open to ideas. With what I am trying to express, I have no idea how else to say it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The best answer is a broadened example.

There is a bus crash on 2/25/12. There are a great number of injuries and fatalities. Charon's Obol experiences an activation event when the bus crashes.

Someone on the bus is significant to the state of the future world. at
this point, the Foundation does not know who it is.

Let's say it lands Tails.

The newspaper on 2/26/12 states that among the fatalities is Tom Smith, a local college student; the coin reads "fast dog" or somesuch. Story over; the world goes on and the flip is logged as having "multiple possible subjects"

Now let's talk heads.

The newspaper on 2/26/12 states that among the injured is a college student in a medically induced coma. 3 months later he leaves the hospital. as fate would have it, the student is pre-med. when the nobel prize for medicine is given out in 2041, Dr. Tomas Smith wins for his pioneering work in transplant technology. His research brought the organ rejection rate down to .025%. He often talks about his life changing event where he narrowly avoided death in a bus. Funny that the coin read "acceptance" when it flipped. Recorded entries are poured through, and the heads activation on the date of the crash with it's applicable message is logged in reference to Dr. Thomas Smith.

IMPORTANT FACT: The crash and the flip in either result are concurrent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty good- clear and also interesting- but I really, really don't like this sentence:

The item acts as an indicator of nothing less than the fulfillment of destiny by significant persons.

Can you replace it with something or just take it out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A 'person of significance' is defined as a person who is destined to influence events or culture directly and globally. Causality study 1289-Alpha has determined that the coin is neither portentous of nor causal to the death or survival of a person of significance. The item acts as an indicator of nothing less than the fulfillment of destiny by significant persons.

What.

I like the article, especially the list of activation events. Just…how did the Foundation go about determining the "destiny" that must be fulfilled by the subjects of the activation events? Would it be better to simply refer to them as persons who have significantly influenced human history (or something else, I don't know)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I must say, I like this thing's personality. The choice of events it displays are interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well played! You're right, I never EVER would have seen that coming at all! Upvoted just for that reference! I was on the fence before but you just sold me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great to see this back and in good working condition. I like the list of events, though it seems skewed more toward pop culture rather than political or historical figures. I certainly had fun running to Wikipedia to look them all up though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suppose the foundation would want a timepiece connected with this thing that's more accurate than an average cell phone… new clock it is.

Also; Angus mentioned the suspension. i think i'll keep that in, but change the reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Vaguely reminds me of some of my own work- I'm a sucker for historical scavenger hunts. I like how the coin has peculiar ideas about what people and events are important.

Satellite-synchronized clock seems weird- in fact, satellites make lousy timepieces because of relativity. I'd suggest having an atomic clock on site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took your wording suggestion to heart in the description; feels like it shook out a small bit of clunk; thanks!

The activations are person-centric. While it would not flip over the moon landing, it may very well have flipped when Kennedy was shot.

Date: November 22, 1963
Result: Tails (indicates expiration, subject identified immediately)
Translation: a phrase; translated as "grass patch" or "green garden"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
well look at you and your due diligence!

+ You have them mostly right...
-Mostly.

Feb. 3: Correct event! It's never been determined if it actually referred to Buddy Holly; it could be Richie Valens or J.P. "The Big Bopper" Richardson. hell, could've been their pilot. In my headcanon the coin flipped when Waylon Jennings gave his plane ticket to the Big Bopper, but only myself and the obol know that.

June 12: Spoiler!

all others you're spot on!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The chamber is affixed with a satellite-synchronized 24-hour clock set to Greenwich Mean Time. The coin is secured centrally within a free-spinning armature, both ends of which are held in place by way of magnetic levitation to assure against friction.

These procedures seem unnecessary and are detracting to me. The video cameras can record the time of events, and I don't understand the need to carefully suspend the coin.

I like that the writing changes. The events listed that it activates over are a bit quirky, does it activate for all major events, i.e. Neil Armstrong stepping on the moon, the Kennedy assassination, etc?

I would recommend using the terms 'obverse' and 'reverse' rather than 'heads' and 'tails'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And now we've gotten to the core of the article, and it's much better for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. The list is a little unusual, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may be a THINGS WHAT TELL THE FUTURE but its still alright. upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In that case…. here's what I've got so far… and I'm just taking a guess on the June 12th one.

[+]My guess/Possible spoiler?[+]
[-]Worth a shot, right?[-]

Feb 3: Buddy Holly
Jun 12: Independence Day of the Philippines????
Mar 30: Reagan assassination attempt
Jan 27: Michael Jackson burns his hair! (Which I must say, I laughed my ass off when I found that, so I hope that's right)
Mar 31: Brandon Lee

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was a tidbit I ran across in research and it is by far my favorite of the five. It PERFECTLY displays what this does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't dislike the idea completely. Sure, there have been plenty of thing what tells the future and plenty of coins to go around. I just don't know if this one has enough original content for myself. I'm not voting for the time being, I'll see if any edits come through to really catch my attention.

Also… I'm good on all the historical references with the exception of the June 12th incident… Just not sure what that is a reference to.

item.title: ..and we're back.
item.content:encoded:
After a severe de-clunking, I've re-posted this coin. Hope you like. Thanks to the commenters in the previous incarnation and in the forums.
UPDATE: New pic due to issues.
Obol photo by Marie Lan-Nguyen (2011)
Photo used under Creative Commons 2.5 Generic License
